title: lindsaea aculcata
side_effects: Lindsaea aculcata can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high saponin content. In some cases, excessive consumption may lead to allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems. Improper use of Lindsaea aculcata may also cause cardiovascular issues, such as palpitations, hypertension, and bradycardia, especially in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. Furthermore, prolonged use or high doses may contribute to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalances.
